resposta:
solution: ```class Solution:
    def mostPoints(self, questions: List[List[int]]) -> int:
        n = len(questions)
        dp = [0] * (n + 1)
        for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
            points, brain = questions[i]
            next_pos = i + brain + 1
            if next_pos >= n:
                dp[i] = max(points, dp[i + 1])
            else:
                dp[i] = max(points + dp[next_pos], dp[i + 1])
        return dp[0]
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Dynamic programming from right to left. dp[i] stores the maximum points obtainable from question i onward. For each question, choose the better of solving it (points + dp[i+brain+1]) or skipping it (dp[i+1]).
